blueprintArgument.claim: Unregulated AI systems perpetuate and amplify systemic bias in high-stakes decisions
blueprintArgument.reasoning: Peer-reviewed studies document disparate impact in AI-driven hiring, lending, housing, and criminal justice tools — disproportionately harming minorities and low-income individuals
blueprintArgument.impact: Millions of people face discriminatory outcomes annually from opaque algorithmic systems with no accountability mechanism or avenue for redress

blueprintArgument.claim: Absent regulation, advanced AI deployment outpaces safety research and creates systemic risk
blueprintArgument.reasoning: AI labs face competitive pressure to deploy before safety benchmarks are met — without external regulatory mandates, no single actor has incentive to slow down unilaterally
blueprintArgument.impact: Deployment of insufficiently tested high-capability AI systems risks cascading failures in critical infrastructure, financial systems, and autonomous weapons contexts

blueprintArgument.claim: Heavy federal AI regulation causes the US to cede AI leadership to less regulated adversaries
blueprintArgument.reasoning: Stringent ex-ante regulation raises compliance costs, delays deployment, and drives AI investment offshore — particularly to China where regulation is strategic rather than restrictive
blueprintArgument.impact: Loss of US AI leadership has cascading national security implications and undermines the ability to set global AI safety norms
